calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-14-2025 Set For Trial On Sep-14-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-20-2025 Continued To Aug-27-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jul-10-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Nissan North America, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is denied. As Nissan acknowledges, its position-that Plaintiff's warranty claims are so intertwined with its contract with the dealer who sold Plaintiff the car that Plaintiff should be compelled to arbitrate those claims notwithstanding that Nissan is not a signatory to Plaintiff's arbitration agreement with the dealer-relies on the viability of Felisilda v. FCA US LLC (2020) 53 Cal.App.5th 486. Felisilda has now been overruled by Ford Motor Warranty Cases (Cal., July 3, 2025, No. S279969) 2025 WL 1830882, which holds that car buyers are not estopped by a buyer-dealer arbitration agreement from pursuing buyer-manufacturer claims in court.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jun-04-2025 Continued To Jul-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
